preview_text:
The model rates Belgium as a modest favorite at 38% win probability, but the gap to Iran stands at only 7 percentage points. The sportsbooks, by contrast, see Belgium as a strong favorite at 68%, reflecting a far wider margin of confidence in the European side's chances.

The model's spread suggests a competitive, closely matched encounter. A draw at 32% is nearly as likely as a Belgium victory, and Iran's 31% win probability indicates the match could plausibly go either direction. The three outcomes remain relatively balanced, pointing to genuine uncertainty in the underlying quality gap between the teams.

Model and market diverge substantially here. The model places Belgium's win probability 30 percentage points below the sportsbook consensus, while sportsbooks elevate Iran's chances to just 12% compared to the model's 31%. Polymarket prices align closely with the stripped sportsbook view. The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment is correct; the discrepancy is large enough to merit attention from users monitoring the fixture.
